target_entity.name: [hygiene-2026-04-22/bug-evidence] Receipt failure auto-filer uses different identities for counting and dedupe
target_entity.summary: Concrete manifestation of the bug-evidence authority cluster. The receipt auto-filer counts failures by failure_code plus node label, but dedupes existing bugs by the full signature tag tuple including a fingerprint. Evidence: canonical bug list contains two open bugs titled Repeated receipt failure: sandbox_error for the same wave0_integrated_design node: BUG-39D02693 after 3 failures and BUG-7C5D8AE4 after 4 failures. Their common tags match on auto-filed, failure_code:sandbox_error, job_label:wave0_integrated_design, failure_category:sandbox_error, provider:unknown, and model:unknown, but the signature tags differ. Code evidence: runtime/receipt_store.py:298-318 builds signature_tags including signature:<fingerprint>; line 318 searches existing bugs by the entire tag tuple; lines 320-330 count receipts only by failure_code and label; lines 334-348 file a new bug when that count crosses threshold. Why it matters: the backlog fragments one operational failure into multiple bugs, while clusters paper over the split instead of preventing duplicate authority rows. Suspected root cause: the identity used for auto-file dedupe is stricter than the identity used for failure aggregation. Suggested next step: define a stable source_issue_id or canonical failure identity based on the aggregation key and upsert/update the existing bug instead of filing a new row when only the fingerprint changes.

target_entity.name: [hygiene-2026-04-22/bug-evidence] Receipt failure auto-filer uses different identities for counting and dedupe
target_entity.summary: Concrete manifestation of the bug-evidence authority cluster. The receipt auto-filer counts failures by failure_code plus node label, but dedupes existing bugs by the full signature tag tuple including a fingerprint. Evidence: canonical bug list contains two open bugs titled Repeated receipt failure: sandbox_error for the same wave0_integrated_design node: BUG-39D02693 after 3 failures and BUG-7C5D8AE4 after 4 failures. Their common tags match on auto-filed, failure_code:sandbox_error, job_label:wave0_integrated_design, failure_category:sandbox_error, provider:unknown, and model:unknown, but the signature tags differ. Code evidence: runtime/receipt_store.py:298-318 builds signature_tags including signature:<fingerprint>; line 318 searches existing bugs by the entire tag tuple; lines 320-330 count receipts only by failure_code and label; lines 334-348 file a new bug when that count crosses threshold. Why it matters: the backlog fragments one operational failure into multiple bugs, while clusters paper over the split instead of preventing duplicate authority rows. Suspected root cause: the identity used for auto-file dedupe is stricter than the identity used for failure aggregation. Suggested next step: define a stable source_issue_id or canonical failure identity based on the aggregation key and upsert/update the existing bug instead of filing a new row when only the fingerprint changes.

target_entity.name: workflow execution still emits route.unhealthy for provider capacity, so receipts repeat and the signal looks like rate limiting
target_entity.summary: `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/_workflow_execution.py` returns `reason_code` / `failure_code` `route.unhealthy` when the load balancer says the provider has no slot. `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/failure_classifier.py` then classifies that same code as `FailureCategory.RATE_LIMIT` with retry advice to fail over again. That is the concrete receipt-churning path behind BUG-EAD36E8A: a capacity rejection is recorded and healed like a generic rate-limit event instead of being surfaced as provider saturation or capacity pressure.

target_entity.name: route.unhealthy is overloaded across provider capacity and route-health failure, so the classifier cannot tell saturation from degradation
target_entity.summary: `route.unhealthy` is carrying two different authorities. `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/_workflow_execution.py` emits it when the load balancer says a provider has no slot, `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/execution_backends.py` also uses it for provider capacity, and `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/failure_classifier.py` classifies it as `FailureCategory.RATE_LIMIT`. That collapses provider saturation, circuit-open state, and actual route-health degradation into one retry lane, so receipts and healers cannot distinguish a full provider from a broken one. Existing open bug BUG-EAD36E8A shows the repeated receipt symptom; this bug is the semantic overload that keeps generating it.

target_entity.name: workflow execution still emits route.unhealthy for provider capacity, so receipts repeat and the signal looks like rate limiting
target_entity.summary: `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/_workflow_execution.py` returns `reason_code` / `failure_code` `route.unhealthy` when the load balancer says the provider has no slot. `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/failure_classifier.py` then classifies that same code as `FailureCategory.RATE_LIMIT` with retry advice to fail over again. That is the concrete receipt-churning path behind BUG-EAD36E8A: a capacity rejection is recorded and healed like a generic rate-limit event instead of being surfaced as provider saturation or capacity pressure.

[hygiene-2026-04-22/bug-evidence] Receipt failure auto-filer uses different identities for counting and dedupe
target_entity.summary: Concrete manifestation of the bug-evidence authority cluster. The receipt auto-filer counts failures by failure_code plus node label, but dedupes existing bugs by the full signature tag tuple including a fingerprint. Evidence: canonical bug list contains two open bugs titled Repeated receipt failure: sandbox_error for the same wave0_integrated_design node: BUG-39D02693 after 3 failures and BUG-7C5D8AE4 after 4 failures. Their common tags match on auto-filed, failure_code:sandbox_error, job_label:wave0_integrated_design, failure_category:sandbox_error, provider:unknown, and model:unknown, but the signature tags differ. Code evidence: runtime/receipt_store.py:298-318 builds signature_tags including signature:<fingerprint>; line 318 searches existing bugs by the entire tag tuple; lines 320-330 count receipts only by failure_code and label; lines 334-348 file a new bug when that count crosses threshold. Why it matters: the backlog fragments one operational failure into multiple bugs, while clusters paper over the split instead of preventing duplicate authority rows. Suspected root cause: the identity used for auto-file dedupe is stricter than the identity used for failure aggregation. Suggested next step: define a stable source_issue_id or canonical failure identity based on the aggregation key and upsert/update the existing bug instead of filing a new row when only the fingerprint changes.

target_entity.name: Worker docker invocation does not propagate CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; macOS .credentials.json is empty (Keychain-only) so claude binary fails Not logged in
target_entity.summary:
End-to-end diagnostic from launch-path verification 2026-04-24:

Manual repro of the worker invocation succeeds:
  docker run --rm --user 1100:1100 \
    --tmpfs /home/praxis-agent/.claude:uid=1100,gid=1100,mode=755 \
    -v /Users/nate/.claude/.credentials.json:/home/praxis-agent/.claude/.credentials.json:ro \
    -v /Users/nate/.claude.json:/home/praxis-agent/.claude.json:ro \
    -e CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN -e HOME=/home/praxis-agent \
    praxis-claude:latest \
    bash -c "echo what is 2+2 | claude -p --output-format json --model claude-sonnet-4-6"
  -> returns success with cost tracking and result text

Worker invocation through SandboxRuntime.execute_command (workflow_832e40bf1b77 step_3 anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6) fails with sandbox_error and stdout: "Not logged in - Please run /login". Earlier runs on opus-4-7 (workflow_043c4932b32d step_1) failed with credential_error and stdout: "API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials request_id=req_011CaPSb...". Two distinct failure modes from the same dispatch path.

Key observations:
  1. macOS .credentials.json is a stub (Keychain holds the actual token); mount alone does not provide auth
  2. CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env-forwarding is required for macOS hosts
  3. _cli_auth_env_forward is called from sandbox_runtime line 1154 with session_provider_slug from session.metadata.provider_slug
  4. sandbox_metadata at execution_backends:748 sets provider_slug correctly
  5. Manual test with identical mount/user/tmpfs/env pattern succeeds, so the docker invocation shape is correct

Probable root: env propagation chain from the launching shell (which has CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) through the workflow runtime to the docker invocation drops the var somewhere, OR the workflow worker daemon was started in a context that lacks the env var. Need to instrument _cli_auth_env_forward to log what it forwards (or doesnt) and trace the actual docker -e flags emitted for one failing job.

Separately: the failure surfaces only after the launch-path lie was fixed (commit e5b8496a). Before that, ok:True with empty run_id hid the entire issue.
